5824	  restriction \((-)|_f\) is the exact pullback \(f^*\), so the finite-presentation and

5826	  \emph{favorable} (open-immersion) case of the base-change comparison, in which the map

5830	  definitional equality, and --- crucially --- that the dual is \emph{not sectionwise}.
5831	  Unlike the tensor product, whose value is computed fibrewise
5832	  (\((M \otimes N)(V) = M(V) \otimes N(V)\)), the dual evaluates to a Hom over an entire
5833	  down-set: for an open \(V \subseteq U\),

5839	  \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\), \emph{not} a single fibre \(M(V) \to \mathcal{O}(V)\). Consequently
5840	  the assertion that restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes with the dual does
5841	  \emph{not} reduce to a single ground-ring reconciliation. The proof proceeds in two
5842	  stages: a preliminary adjunction-uniqueness rewrite (stage~H1) that re-presents the
5843	  pulled-back dual in pushforward form, followed by the resolution of the resulting
5844	  \emph{residual} into \emph{two} legs, carried out objectwise in the open \(V\) (with

5848	  presented in the \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\)-form inherited from the abstract
5849	  definition of the restriction functor, and no sectionwise comparison can begin against
5850	  that opaque form. We first rewrite it into a \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)-form: the
5851	  restriction-along-an-open-immersion pullback and the pushforward along the structure-ring
5852	  map \(\beta\) are both left adjoints to one and the same functor, so by uniqueness of left

5854	  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) --- they are canonically isomorphic, and this isomorphism

5863	  \emph{not} the original Step-4 isomorphism --- that legs (A) and (B) below resolve; legs
5864	  (A) and (B) are the content remaining \emph{after} the H1 rewrite, not a standalone
5865	  two-leg split of the whole step.

5867	  \emph{Leg (A) --- slice-site Hom base-change (Beck--Chevalley).} The domain presheaf must
5868	  first be transported across the open immersion. Because \(f\) is an open immersion, its
5869	  direct-image functor on open sets is fully faithful with image
5870	  the down-set \(\{W : W \le U\}\); this carries the restricted domain
5871	  \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) to the

5874	  slices. This is a genuine slice-site Hom base-change --- a Beck--Chevalley square on the
5875	  internal hom across the fully-faithful open-immersion functor, restricted to the down-set
5876	  of \(fV\) --- \emph{not} a fibrewise identity.
5877	
5878	  \emph{Leg (B) --- ground-ring reconciliation.} The codomain of the internal hom is in
5879	  both cases the structure sheaf. Once leg (A) has matched the domains, the unit codomain
5880	  is reconciled along the open-immersion ring isomorphism

5882	  \mathcal{O}_X(fV) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y(V)\): restriction of scalars along the
5883	  ring \emph{isomorphism} \(\beta_V\) is an equivalence, and its ModuleCat-level
5884	  shadow on duals is exactly \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}, carrying the
5885	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(fV)\)-linear dual onto the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear dual,

5887	  ring-isomorphism tensor equivalence (\cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}).

5890	  realised together by a single \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear isomorphism
5891	  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\) (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}), built
